sections.paragraph:
πατρώνυμος, ον named after the father (QDAP 1, ’31, 155 [Gaza, III a.d.]) IRo ins (on the subject matter cp. Eph 3:14f; s. Lghtf. on IRo ins), where God the Father is meant.—DELG s.v. ὄνομα.

QDAP QDAP = Quarterly of the Department of Antiquities in Palestine—List 6

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 789
